title: Starlight Derelict
setting: The SS Starlight, a derelict deep-space research vessel drifting in orbit around a dead planet. The crew vanished after an experiment with alien organic matter went wrong. Something still moves in the dark corridors.

characters.name: Dr. Althea Ramos
characters.description: The station's chief xenobiologist. Her final logs describe a 'living membrane' that consumes everything it touches.

characters.name: The Grey Matter
characters.description: An alien biological entity that spreads like a sentient mold, absorbing organic material and mimicking human voices.

items.name: Access Keycard
items.description: A standard crew access card. It should open most doors on the station.

items.name: Plasma Cutter
items.description: A heavy-duty cutter used for hull repairs. Whines ominously when activated.

items.name: Data Crystal
items.description: A small crystalline storage device containing the station's final research logs and the creature's weakness.

items.name: Emergency Power Cell
items.description: A high-capacity battery used to start the auxiliary power system.

items.name: Override Chip
items.description: A specialized chip that can bypass the docking clamp's auto-lock sequence.

items.name: Oxygen Tank
items.description: A full-tank portable oxygen supply. Essential for EVA.

scenes.name: Airlock
scenes.prose: You float in the inner airlock chamber. The metal walls are scarred and slimy. An inner door leads deeper into the station. An EVA suit hangs on a hook, its helmet cracked. The outer hatch is sealed by an automatic clamp mechanism.

lookDescriptions.eva suit: The oxygen feed is severed. It's useless without an air supply.
lookDescriptions.clamp control: A magnetic locking clamp preventing the outer hatch from opening. It requires an override signal.
lookDescriptions.inner door: A standard crew door with a card reader.

scenes.name: Maintenance Bay
scenes.prose: A cramped workshop filled with tools and spare parts. The floor is sticky with a grey, fibrous substance. A power conduit runs along the ceiling, sparking intermittently. A door to the crew quarters lies to the north.

lookDescriptions.power conduit: The main power feed to the station is shorted. It needs a reset after being cut.
lookDescriptions.sticky floor: The grey fuzz spreads from the vents. It pulses faintly.

scenes.name: Crew Quarters
scenes.prose: Rows of sleeping pods line the walls. Most are empty, their sheets stained with the same grey residue. A personal locker is ajar, spilling clothes. The air smells of damp earth.

lookDescriptions.locker: Inside you find a faded family photo and a bottle of whiskey. Nothing useful.

scenes.name: Engineering Bay
scenes.prose: The station's power core hums faintly, its coolant pipes leaking a viscous fluid. A primary control console is dark. An emergency power terminal sits near the wall. A shut-down valve for the coolant system is nearby, coated in the grey growth.

lookDescriptions.power core: The reactor is stable but output is minimal without a main power feed.
lookDescriptions.shut-down valve: The valve handle is encased in a thick layer of the alien growth. You'll need to cut through it.

scenes.name: Laboratory
scenes.prose: This wing housed xenobiology research. Workstations are overturned. Petri dishes and sample vials litter the floor. In the center, a containment chamber has been shattered. A terminal flickers with a warning screen. A data crystal rests on a console.

lookDescriptions.containment breach: Something big broke out of here.
lookDescriptions.terminal: It requests an access code, but the override chip might bypass it.

scenes.name: Storage Room
scenes.prose: Supplies for the long mission: food packs, tools, and spare parts. Everything is covered in the creeping grey matter. A sealed escape pod bay lies to the east, its door requiring manual release from inside. A communications array panel is on the north wall, its transmitter fried.

lookDescriptions.escape pod: The hatch is sealed. Once you start the ejection sequence, there's no turning back.
lookDescriptions.communications array: The transmitter needs a data upload to send a distress signal and unlock the pod.

scenes.name: Escape Pod Bay
scenes.prose: A single escape pod waits in the small launch bay. The cockpit canopy is clear. Inside, there are three seats and a control panel that asks for an authorization code. The pod will launch once the station's docking clamps are released and you have a secured oxygen supply.

lookDescriptions.control panel: It displays: 'AUTHORIZATION REQUIRED - INSERT OVERRIDE CHIP'.
lookDescriptions.seats: Safety harnesses are in place. The trip will be rough.

puzzles.result: The inner door clicks and slides open with a hiss of equalizing pressure.
puzzles.blockedMessage: The door is locked. Swipe an access card to proceed.

puzzles.result: The cutter burns through the fibrous mass. You turn the valve, stopping the coolant leak and restoring partial power to engineering.
Sparks fly as station power comes online. Lights flicker on throughout the ship. The communications array in Storage now has power.

The terminal accepts the crystal and downloads its contents. It displays: 'DOCKING CLAMPS OVERRIDE CODE: 7-7-7'. The escape pod now accepts the override chip. An airlock cycle completes, clearing the grey matter from the pod bay entrance.
puzzles.blockedMessage: The pod bay hatch is sealed. You need to activate the terminal and clear the airlock.

puzzles.action: use override-chip on panel and attach oxygen-tank
puzzles.result: Override chip accepted. Oxygen supply secured. The pod detaches from the station and rockets away into the void. The SS Starlight becomes a tomb once more — this time with you gone.

winCondition.description: The pod clears the station's hull and enters a stable trajectory. You watch the derelict recede into the starfield. You've survived the Starlight Derelict.
